input.task: Explain dependency pinning and why exact versions are recommended for production and CI.

content.answer_preview: Dependency pinning refers to specifying exact versions of packages in a project's dependencies (Page 36). This practice is crucial for production and Continuous Integration (CI) environments, as recommended on Page 39. The reason behind this recommendation lies in the sensitivity of Machine Learning systems to version changes. For instance, a minor update in NumPy can alter numerical results, while an upgrade in scikit-learn may change model behavior (Page 37). By using exact versions, such as n

final_answer: Dependency pinning refers to specifying exact versions of packages in a project's dependencies (Page 36). This practice is crucial for production and Continuous Integration (CI) environments, as recommended on Page 39. The reason behind this recommendation lies in the sensitivity of Machine Learning systems to version changes. For instance, a minor update in NumPy can alter numerical results, while an upgrade in scikit-learn may change model behavior (Page 37). By using exact versions, such as numpy==1.24.3 and pandas==2.0.3, the project ensures reproducibility and consistency of its results across different runs (Page 36 & 39).
